notes: Skeleton reads this to decide which wizard stages to run for a given goal. The 'difficulty' field on a catalog entry (or the tunnel runWizard payload) selects a tier. When not provided, auto_classify.defaults_to wins. Tuning this file is the LEARN-loop seam for performance vs. quality.

L1.rationale: Simple CRUD / single-view / static content. One LLM call, no planner, no CLI agentic session, no autofix, NO Visual Director brief. Evaluator still runs to feed the rubric ledger, but its result does not trigger a fix turn. Typical wall-clock 30-90s. SL.5: estimate baseline = $0.05/run (gpt-4o-mini single turn ~3k in + ~3k out). VD note: skip_visual_director=true because the fast-path's single-call budget cannot absorb a VD preamble; L1 goals are simple enough that VD offers little uplift.

L2.rationale: Default tier. Planner scopes the spec, generator uses CLI-first with API fallback, evaluator critiques and triggers one autofix cycle. VD runs (brief preamble + mockup). SL.5: estimate baseline = $0.15/run (planner + generator + evaluator + one fix cycle).

L3.rationale: Framework-heavy or stateful goals. Deep planning runs ahead of the simple planner, generator gets extended budget, multiple autofix cycles allowed. VD runs with full budget (brief + mockup + iterations allowed). SL.5: estimate baseline = $0.40/run (deep plan + generator 32k + evaluator + 2 fix cycles, higher variance).

auto_classify.notes: Used ONLY when the caller does not pass an explicit difficulty. Catalog-based flywheel runs always pass difficulty explicitly and bypass this.
